texto: If you're in love, love to the limit; and if you hate, why hate like the devil and if it's a fight you're in, get where it's hottest and fight like hell - if you don't life's not worth the living.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Abraham Grace Merritt: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “If you're in love, love to the limit; and if you hate, why hate like the devil and if it's a figh…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, fighting, hatred pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de hatred e vida.

texto: We strike our blow, even as Pierre has said. We strike at the coppice that you so desire. We strike there because it is the very heart of the forest. There the secret life of the forest runs at full tide. We know - and you know! Something that, destroyed, will take the heart out of the forest - will make it know us for its masters."("Women Of The Woods")
explicacao: Citação de Abraham Grace Merritt sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “We strike our blow, even as Pierre has said. We strike at the coppice that you so desire. We stri…” articula amor, dryad, dryads em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como dryads e nature.

texto: From the first she showed a curious sensitivity to what, I suppose, may be called the 'influences' of the place. She said it 'smelled' of ghosts and warlocks.
explicacao: Citação de Abraham Grace Merritt sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “From the first she showed a curious sensitivity to what, I suppose, may be called the 'influences…” articula reflexao, pulp-fiction, sci-fi em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como sci-fi.
